# Copilot Instructions

- Stack: TanStack Start + Vite + React 19 + TypeScript; file-based routing via TanStack Router; Tailwind CSS v4 plugin; Zustand for client state; React Flow (@xyflow) for workflow canvas; TanStack Query wired for SSR.
- Working rules: no manual reformatting; do not reorder/sort imports or cull unused code; do not run Biome—user handles lint/format.
- Componentization: when building or updating a page/route, separate code into manageable focused components/hooks instead of creating or expanding monolithic page files.
- Commands: prefer package scripts (README start is outdated). `npm run dev` (Vite, port 3000), `npm run build`, `npm run preview`, `npm run test` (vitest run), `npm run lint|format|check` (Biome).
- Env: set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (used by TanStack AI hooks).
- Routing shell: [src/routes/__root.tsx](src/routes/__root.tsx) defines head tags, mounts global CSS, renders the shared Header, and hosts TanStack Devtools plugins (router panel, query, store, AI). Do not touch `routeTree.gen.ts`; add/modify routes under [src/routes](src/routes).
- Router wiring: [src/router.tsx](src/router.tsx) builds the router with TanStack Query context from [src/integrations/tanstack-query/root-provider.tsx](src/integrations/tanstack-query/root-provider.tsx) and calls `setupRouterSsrQueryIntegration` for SSR/query hydration. Path alias `@/*` -> `src/*` (see [tsconfig.json](tsconfig.json)).
- Devtools: Root shell loads `TanStackDevtools` with router, query, store, and AI plugins from [src/lib/ai-devtools.tsx](src/lib/ai-devtools.tsx) and [src/lib/demo-store-devtools.tsx](src/lib/demo-store-devtools.tsx).
- Styling: Tailwind utility classes plus global styles in [src/styles.css](src/styles.css); no CSS modules. Keep class ordering as-is (let Biome/linters handle style changes).
- Workflow canvas: [src/components/workflow/WorkflowCanvas.tsx](src/components/workflow/WorkflowCanvas.tsx) seeds start/end + default node/edge when empty, blocks connections into start or out of end, and prevents deleting start/end. Buttons add generic or condition nodes; edges default to animated `custom-edge`; toggle shows raw JSON for the current graph. Save uses Zustand store and navigates back to `/workflow`.
- Workflow nodes/edges: Start/End nodes expose only source/target handles ([src/components/workflow/StartNode.tsx](src/components/workflow/StartNode.tsx), [src/components/workflow/EndNode.tsx](src/components/workflow/EndNode.tsx)); `ConditionNode` carries `data.input` and edge removal controls; `CustomNode` supports rename/remove and pruning edges; `CustomEdge` deletes on click; `DataEdge` displays `data.input`.
- Workflow persistence: [src/lib/workflow-store.ts](src/lib/workflow-store.ts) persists to `loan-workflows`; `addWorkflow` trims/validates name, auto-selects the new workflow, and stores optional `sourceInstanceId`; `updateWorkflow` also enforces trimmed names; `getWorkflowList` sorts by name then creation time.
- Loan setup snapshots: [src/lib/loan-setup-store.ts](src/lib/loan-setup-store.ts) persists to `loan-workflow-setups`; `addSetup` trims channel codes, normalizes document requirements (deduped, DOC- prefix, defaults to NRC/PAYSLIP), clones interest rate plans, and captures scorecard/workflow/repayment IDs plus bureau flags. `updateSetup` re-normalizes and preserves prior docs when none provided. Migrations exist but backward compatibility is not critical.
- Scorecards: [src/lib/scorecard-store.ts](src/lib/scorecard-store.ts) persists to `loan-scorecard` with seeded `defaultScoreCard`. Normalization humanizes field descriptions and groups legacy flat rules; removal restores the default if empty.
- Score engine: [src/lib/scorecard-engine.ts](src/lib/scorecard-engine.ts) infers field types from operators, parses numeric/boolean inputs, skips missing values, caps total at `maxScore`, and maps to `riskGrade` + minimal docs list.
- Loan applications: [src/lib/loan-application-store.ts](src/lib/loan-application-store.ts) persists to `loan-applications`; `addApplication` trims strings, clamps numeric fields to non-negative, generates `applicationNo` as `${productCode}-${Date.now().toString(36).toUpperCase()}`, initializes workflow history, and stores bureau metadata. `updateStatus`, `advanceWorkflowStage`, and `resetWorkflowProgress` update status and history with timestamps.
- Loan setup UI: [src/routes/loan/setup/index.tsx](src/routes/loan/setup/index.tsx) orchestrates product config, channels, scorecard + workflow selection, repayment plan, bureau flags, and disbursement settings; calls `evaluateScoreCard`, saves snapshots via `useLoanSetupStore.addSetup`, and links to builder pages. It seeds a default `loanProductSetup`, normalizes score inputs per selected scorecard, maps document requirements into flat `DocumentRequirement` records, and only sends bureau fields when bureau checks are enabled.
- Workflow routes: [src/routes/workflow/index.tsx](src/routes/workflow/index.tsx) lists saved workflows; [src/routes/workflow/setup.tsx](src/routes/workflow/setup.tsx) hosts the canvas for create/edit; [src/routes/workflow/$workflowId.tsx](src/routes/workflow/$workflowId.tsx) renders details.
- Loan application routes: [src/routes/loan/applications/create.tsx](src/routes/loan/applications/create.tsx) builds new applications from selected setup and optional scorecard evaluation; [src/routes/loan/applications/$applicationId.tsx](src/routes/loan/applications/$applicationId.tsx) shows details; [src/routes/loan/applications/index.tsx](src/routes/loan/applications/index.tsx) lists them.
- Business logic doc policy: whenever the user asks to change, update, or revise business logic/business logics, always edit [src/docs/business-logic.md](src/docs/business-logic.md) as part of the change.
- Demo content: Files prefixed with `demo` (including under [src/components/demo...](src/components), [src/routes/demo](src/routes/demo), and [src/lib/score-card.ts](src/lib/score-card.ts)) are disposable examples.
- Shadcn: Per [.cursorrules](.cursorrules), add components via `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add <component>` (e.g., button).

If anything here is unclear or incomplete, tell me what to expand or refine.
